calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-05-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mike Woods appeared by zoom for Robert McDill and Anastasia McDill (personal representatives).
Kimberly Nielson appeared by zoom for Carlton Jackson (beneficiary of the Gertie Foster Estate).
Glenda August appeared by zoom for the heirs of the Preston Segura Estate.

Mr. Woods stated that there is no reason why this estate should not be closed. Mrs. august and Mrs. Neilson have no objections. The order to show cause and status on close of estate are off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Apr-29-2024 Continued To May-20-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-15-298786 Estate of Gertie Victoria Foster and
PES-21-304575 Estate of Ollie Bernard Foster.

Appearances: Michael Woods for and with Robert McDill and Jean McDill.
Kimberly Nelson appeared by zoom for Carlton Jackson (beneficiary/objector).
Glenda August appeared by zoom for the Heirs of Preston Segura.

The court noted that the number of disputes is getting smaller and smaller. Mrs. August stated that there only three disputes left (paragraph 12/$40,000.00 settlement payment; paragraph 13/executor fees; paragraph 21/Mr. Hightower's $23,000.00 bill), that they are working diligently to get this resolved and that she believes these three remaining disputes will need to go to the court for a decision. Mr. Woods stated that there are no examples of purposeful delay on behalf of his clients and that Mr. Hightower's fees should come from Mr. Keith's statutory fees. All counsel indicated that they are ready to submit the matter and get this resolved/put this to rest. The Amended Account for the Eight Year Period of Estate, etc., and the Final Account and Report of Executors, etc. in Gertie's estate are under submission. Status on close of estate in Ollie's estate is continued to May 20, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on termination.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Feb-28-2024 Continued To Apr-29-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michael Woods, Esq., for and with Jean McDill and Robert McDill, all appearing in person, Petitioners.
Kimberly Neilson, Esq., via Zoom video conference, for Carlton Jackson, Beneficiary.
Glenda August, Esq., via Zoom video conference, for and with Preston Segura II, Shayla Segura, Whitney Segura, and Tanya Brown, Heirs.

At the court's query, Mr. Woods reports that he intends to file a petition for distribution within three weeks with a request for a reserve to handle the related Estate matter in Louisiana. There is discussion of the possible hearing date that Mr. Woods' petition is likely to receive from the clerk at the time of filing, likely forty-five (45) days from the date of filing. Ms. August reminds the court of the delays with closing this matter and asks that the court consider hearing the matter sooner. After discussion of the preceding, the court and the parties agree to schedule the hearing on Mr. Woods' petition for Apr-29-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The court advises Mr. Woods that when his petition is ready to file, he should indicate to the clerk that Judge Moody has approved his petition to be heard on Apr-29-2024. The court then confirms that all parties agree to release $20,000 to Beneficiary Carlton Jackson, as stipulated. Ms. Neilson advises that she intends to forward the proposed order thereon to the court via email. The court then recognizes Ms. August's status on renewal of her pro hac vice fee which is currently set for Mar-13-2024. The court advises that it intends to advance the preceding to consider it today. No objection to the court's suggestion is presented. The court discusses Mr. Woods' intention to file a substitution of attorney in the related matter of the Estate of Ollie B. Foster.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Apr-29-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Aug-02-2023 Continued To Oct-02-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard: PES-15-298786 The estate of the Gertie Foster and PES-21-304575 Matter of the estate of Ollie Bernard Foster

Appearances; Phillip Keith appearing in person for and with Robert McDill and Jean Anastasia McDill appearing in person (petitioners).
Glenda August appearing by zoom for the heirs Preston Segura II, Tanya Brown, Shayla Segura and Whitney Segura. (beneficiaries).
Kimberly Neilson appearing via zoom for Carlton Jackson (beneficiary).

Discussion was held and all parties are in agreement of the petition. Mrs. Neilson stated that her primary concern is to move this forward get the petition approved because her client is aging. Mr. Keith spoke of the property being transferred from the Ollie Foster estate to the Gertie Foster estate. Amended Petition and account from eight-year period of estate from May 1, 2015, to December 31, 2022 and report of executors and Status on closing of estate are continued to October 02, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Amended petition for order for preliminary distribution of AT&T Common stock and accrued dividends to the estate of Preston Segura is taken under submission.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Singh: Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jun-07-2023 Continued To Aug-02-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jun-07-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard: PES-15-298786 The estate of the Gertie Foster and PES-21-304575 Matter of the estate of Ollie Bernard Foster

Appearances; Phillip Keith appearing in person for and with Robert McDill and Jean Anastasia McDill appearing on person.
Glenda August appearing by zoom for the heirs Preston Segura II, Tanya Brown, Shayla Segura and Whitney Segura.
Lisa Frisella appearing via zoom for Carlton Jackson (beneficiary).

Mrs. August stated that the repairs to the property will be paid by the estate. Mrs. Friscella stated that the examiners notes relating to the settlement agreement in 2017, Order approving settlement agreement has been satisfied. Mr. Keith reported as to the assets coming into the estate, and that that he will be filing a petition to terminate Ollie's estate due to lack of assets. The Court ordered both order's to show cause re: suspension/removal off calendar. Status on close of estate is continued to July 25, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Petition and account for seven year period of estate from May 01, 2015 to December 31, 2021 is continued to July 25, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Maloney: Not reported.

June 07, 2023
Upon further review based on the Courts Own Motion, Status on close of estate is continued to August 02, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Petition and account for seven-year period of estate from May 01, 2015 to December 31, 2021 is continued to August 02, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Petition for order for preliminary distribution of ATT common stock and accrued dividends to the estate of Preston Segura is continued to August 02, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Letters Of Administration
Off Calendar Mar-29-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard; PES-15-298786 Estate of Gertie Victoria Foster and PES-21-304575 The Estate of Ollie Bernard Foster.

Appearances; Phillip Keith appearing in person for and with Robert McDill and for with Jean Anastasia McDill.
Glenda August and Somita Basu appeared by zoom for the heirs of Preston Segura.

Mr. Keith stated that the accounting was e-filed and served, and that he will make sure he will send a copy to the Segura heirs. The Court stated that it is a seven-year accounting describing the affairs of the estate and how much time is needed, and on its own motion continued the hearing date on the accounting from May 31, 2023 to June 07, 2023. The Court also on its own motion advanced the hearing date of April 25, 2023 on Petition to appear as Counsel Pro Hac Vice to today and will grant it today. The Court noted the petitioners' desire to participate fully and stated that it is considering taking the order to show cause re: suspension/removal off calendar. Ms. August stated she did not have enough time to prepare in response to the accounting, that there are several things she is objecting to, and objects to the Order to show cause being taken off calendar. Mr. Keith reported as to the assets coming into the estate, spoke of the Louisiana estate and stated that all the assets in California as far as he knows were held in joint tenancy and have been moved to the estate in Louisiana. Mr. Keith also stated that he may be filing a petition to terminate Ollie's estate due to lack of assets. Status on close of estate for Gertie is off calendar. Status of issuance of letters of administration in Ollie's estate is off calendar. The Orders to show cause re: suspension/removal (in both matters) are continued to June 07, 2023 at 9:00 am in department 204. Status on close of estate in Ollie's estate is also continued to June 07, 2023 for status the ancillary estate.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Maloney. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-29-2023 Continued To Jun-07-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Mar-01-2023 Continued To Mar-29-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Feb-01-2023 Continued To Mar-29-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Letters Of Administration
Off Calendar Feb-01-2023 Continued To Mar-29-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter is called with case no. PES-15-298786; The Estate of Gertie Victoria Foster.

Appearances: Phillip Keith, Esq., in court, for and with Jean Anastasia McDill and Robert McDill (Co-Executors), both present in court. Glenda August, Esq., via Zoom, and Somita Basu, Esq., via Zoom, for Preston Segura (Heirs).

The Court stated that the estate in Gertie Victoria Foster is a mess, that it needs to be untangled, that it needs to be closed, and that it had read what counsel said about the order to appear and his calendar issues. The Court inquired about the path forward. Mr. Keith stated that they have orders in Ollie transferring title in Louisiana and that there is some farmland/ oil and gas producing property going into the estate. Ms. McDill stated that the oil and gas property brings in about $40,000 per month. The Court inquired about an appraiser. Mr. Keith stated that opening Ollie's estate helped with that, that they did not need letters just an order, that all assets in California are held in joint tenancy, that when Ollie died there was no probate and it all passed outside probate, that there are no issue, and that Gertie was the sole beneficiary. The Court stated that the probate examiner's notes seek a supplement signed by the co-executors, identification of decedent's siblings by name including the dates of death of those who are deceased, identification of their issue, and that it needs to make sure no one is missing. Ms. McDill noted roadblocks in obtaining information. The Court stated that it operates on notice and the opportunity to be heard and that the Probate Code is set up to give people their chance. Mr. Keith stated that they were only aware of Preston Segura and Carlton Jackson. Ms. August stated that Ms. Basu has entered the record, that at the last hearing they only talked about the failure to appear, that they were to file written objections, that they wanted to address the AT&T stock that was not turned over, and that they want a full accounting. Discussions are held. Ms. McDill stated that they were to get the estate in Louisiana and that they were to file the accounts. Ms. McDill also stated that after the preliminary distribution of $250,000 that she was not going to make distributions without counsel. Ms. August stated that she came into this matter three years ago, that $125,000.00 went to each client, that pursuant to the will that the stock was to be turned over, and that Mr. Keith put the AT&T stocks in the accounting which should not have been done. The Court noted that Mr. Keith knows how to file a formal petition for approval on further status. Mr. Keith stated that he will ask for more time, that no major issues are left, that the real property was sold, and that the funds are in the estate minus the $100,000 settlement, etc. The Court inquired about the inventory and appraisal. Mr. McDill stated the land would take 2 months and is done, that the mineral rights would take 3 to 4 months, and that the Louisiana lawyer set up the contracts. Ms. McDill noted that they gathered funds and were waiting to give it to the heirs.

At the conclusion of comments, the Court ordered the following:
Status on close in Case No. PES-15-298786; Estate of Gertie Victoria Foster is continued to March 29, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
Status of letters of administration in Case No. PES-21-304575; Estate of Ollie Bernard Foster is continued to March 29, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
The Orders to Show Cause in both cases are continued to March 29, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Orders to Appear in both cases are off calendar.
Mr. Keith is to clear the examiner's notes, file a petition for approval on further report on status of administration/serve it, file the supplement, and inform the probate referee of the new info.
Case No. PES-21-304575; Ollie Bernard Foster is to remain open.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Letters Of Administration
Off Calendar Nov-23-2022 Continued To Feb-01-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. The court notes the delay with this matter since the appointment of the co-Administrators over one (1) year ago. The court vacates the order appointing Jean Anastasia McDill and Robert McDill as co-Administrators and therefore they are suspended. An Order to Appear will be issued to co-Administrators Jean Anastasia McGill and Robert McDill and Attorney Phillip Keith to appear on Feb-01-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why Letters of Administration have not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date. An Order to Show Cause as to why co- Administrators Jean Anastasia McGill and Robert McDill and Attorney Phillip Keith should not be sanctioned $250 each, and An Order to Show Cause as to why the court should not remove co-Administrators Jean Anastasia McGill and Robert McDill is set for Feb-01-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Responses to the Orders to Show Cause are to be filed with the court by Jan-19-2023 and courtesy copies delivered by noon on Jan-20-2023. The status hearing is continued to Feb-01-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Saephan. Not Reported.

***After hearing (12/05/2022): Due to a clerical oversight, the court is not vacating the Nov-22-2021 Order appointing Jean Anastasia McDill and Robert McDill as co-Administrators, as stated above.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Letters Of Administration
Off Calendar Oct-17-2022 Continued To Nov-23-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Phillip Keith, via CourtCall, for Anastasia McDill and Robert McDill, co-Executors; Glenda August, via Zoom, for Preston Segura II and Carlton Jackson, Heirs in related Estate of Preston Segura.

Mr. Keith reports of having an accounting ready to file and that continued substantial income from the Louisiana gas properties has caused delays in finalizing an accounting and inventory and appraisal. Mr. Keith advises that the AT&T stocks continue to earn dividends and that he is still working on claiming Letters in the related Estate of Ollie Foster matter. Ms. August reports that she was retained about thirty (30) days ago to handle the related Estate of Preston Segura and will do her best to move that matter along. Ms. August expresses concerns regarding the position of Preston Segura's children in the settlement agreement.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Nov-23-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Letters Of Administration
Off Calendar Aug-22-2022 Continued To Oct-17-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Nov-22-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Philip S. Keith, Esq. by CourtCall for Anastasia McDill & Robert McDill, Petitioners;
No other appearances by any party or counsel on this matter.

The Court inquired about the need for Ms. McDill to add the name "Jean" in this matter. Mr. Keith responded stating that it should not affect anything in this case. The Court noted that people use different names, and that it did not believe that the matter needed to be renoticed.

The Court then inquired if anyone was appearing to object. No one appeared to object.

There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The will dated August 22, 1984 is admitted to probate. Anastasia McDill and Robert McDill are appointed as the Co-Administrators with the will annexed with full authority. Bond is not required. Isidoor Bornstein is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for December 12, 2022, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 in this matter.

Mr. Keith noted that Ollie Foster's assets will need to be transferred to Gertie Foster, that it involves real property in Louisiana, and that they have counsel in Louisiana. The Court stated that it would like to find out what needs to be done in Gertie Foster's matter. The Court set status on close of the estate in the Estate of Gertie Victoria Foster, related case no. PES-15-298786, for January 10, 2022, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: R. Loo. Matter not reported.
